# Interface Change Request (ICR-260001)

## 1. Request Metadata

- Request ID: `ICR-260001`
- Date: `2026-06-23`
- Requester: `Repository owner (info@aleutheris.com) via assistant`
- Affected project: `Crystord App`
- Priority: `critical`

## 2. Contract Scope

Select all that apply:

- [x] Public API (backend GraphQL contract consumed by the frontend)
- [x] Module/service interface (`api-contract` adapters/queries)
- [ ] Database schema contract
- [ ] Event/message contract
- [x] External config/env contract (`public/config.json` `backendSchemaRange`)

## 3. Current Contract

- Contract identifier: backend GraphQL schema, pinned via `public/config.json` `backendSchemaRange: "^6.0.0"`.
- Current behavior/signature (auth/authz surface only):
  - One-shot `signup(email, password) -> token`; token treated as permanent.
  - `signin` / `signinGoogle` mint a non-expiring token; `signinGoogle` silently links by email.
  - Reads (`retrieve`, `list_labels`, taxonomy reads) usable without a token.
  - Errors surfaced via HTTP `401` / `extensions.code` (e.g. `UNAUTHENTICATED`).
- Current consumers: `src/api-contract/*`, `src/features/auth-entry/*`, `src/ui-shell/*`,
  `src/bootstrap/*`.

## 4. Proposed Change

- Proposed behavior/signature: adopt backend GraphQL schema **8.1.0** as the new pinned contract.
  Auth/authz-relevant deltas:
  - `signup` **removed** → `beginSignup(email)` + `completeSignup(email, code, password, username)`.
  - Tokens become **sessions** (expire ~24h idle / ~30d absolute; revocable via `logout`,
    `revokeAllSessions`, password reset, email change).
  - `signinGoogle` no longer auto-links → `AUTH-GOOGLE-NOT-LINKED`; new `linkGoogle(idToken)`.
  - Almost all reads (incl. taxonomy reads) now **require a Bearer session token**.
  - Error codes now arrive in the GraphQL **`message`** (no `extensions.code` / data-field body).
  - New account-management ops: `me`, `setPassword`, `unlinkAuthMethod`,
    `requestPasswordReset`/`confirmPasswordReset`, `requestEmailChange`/`confirmEmailChange`,
    `deleteMyAccount`.
- Change type: `breaking`
- Why current contract is insufficient: the 8.1.0 deployment removed the old operations; the current
  client's `signup` call and permanent-token assumptions fail outright, blocking **all new sign-ups**.

## 5. Impact Analysis

- Technical impact: `api-contract` (queries, error handling, schema range), `auth-entry`
  (sign-up/sign-in/session/account UI), `ui-shell` (logout control, settings route),
  `workspace-graph`/`workspace-details` (access-level affordance gating).
- Consumer impact: the entire frontend auth path must change; new users cannot register until the
  verify-first flow ships.
- Operational impact: requires `public/config.json` `backendSchemaRange` bump and re-verification of
  the startup `schemaInfo` handshake; demo account must be (re)provisioned by the owner.
- Risks: silent failure of the existing 401/`extensions.code` expiry detector against 8.1.0's
  message-based error transport; expired-session UX regressions; demo session expiry mid-use.

## 6. Compatibility Strategy

- Backward compatibility plan: **none possible** — this is a hard backend cut. Mitigated by the
  startup `schemaInfo` handshake failing fast on an unsupported version (ADR-260013).
- Deprecation path: old auth requirements/ADRs deprecated and superseded (see Links); the
  `email`-as-username shim (`REQ-OR-260013`) is retired in favor of real `username` at
  `completeSignup`.
- Sunset timeline: immediate on adoption of 8.1.0; no dual-version support window.

## 7. Migration Plan

- Step-by-step migration: delivered as the **Authentication & Authorization Epic**, sliced S1→S3
  (see ADR-260054):
  - S1: contract adoption + error mapper + session model + verify-first sign-up + password reset.
  - S2: account-management surface (Google linking, set/unlink method, email change, delete).
  - S3: read-side access-level affordance gating.
- Validation checkpoints: per-slice quality gates (lint, typecheck, unit, e2e) with the
  `schemaInfo == 8.1.0` gate green.
- Rollback plan: redeploy last-known-good artifact pinned to the prior backend; revert
  `backendSchemaRange`. (Note: rollback only viable while a compatible backend is reachable.)

## 8. Verification Plan

- End-to-end behavior checks: verify-first sign-up, sign-in, session-expiry → re-auth, server logout,
  password reset, demo entry, gated reads attach a token.
- Contract/integration tests: `schema-compatibility` against `~8.1.0`; auth operation documents match
  8.1.0; central error-code mapper covers every code in the epic table.
- Observability checks: auth failures, schema-compatibility failures, and demo sign-in failures emit
  structured, actionable diagnostics.

## 9. Decision

- Decision: `approved`
- Human approver: `Repository owner (info@aleutheris.com)`
- Approval date: `2026-06-23`
- Notes: Approved in session while walking the Auth & Authz decision register
  (`AUTH-AUTHZ-EPIC.TEMP.md`). Schema range pinned to `~8.1.0` (patch-only auto-adoption; `8.2.0`+
  requires a new ICR). Taxonomy (Facet→Dimension) and sharing-write migrations are explicitly **out of
  scope** of this ICR and will be raised under their own ICRs/epics.

## 10. Implementation Record

- Linked changeset: `<pending — BI-260054…BI-260061>`
- Release target: Auth & Authz Epic, slice S1 first.
- Post-release review date: `n/a`
